data.command: export PATH="$HOME/go/bin:$HOME/bin:$PATH" && gt prime --hook && gt mail check --inject

message.content:
[GAS TOWN] deacon <- daemon • 2026-02-24T02:56 • patrol

I am Deacon. Start patrol: run gt deacon heartbeat, then check gt hook. If no hook, create mol-deacon-patrol wisp and execute it.

content.content:
? skogtown-wisp-wisp-07qybh [EPIC] · mol-deacon-patrol   [● P2 · HOOKED]
Assignee: deacon · Type: epic
Created: 2026-02-23 · Updated: 2026-02-23

DESCRIPTION
Deacon patrol cycle.

Monitors Gas Town agents (Mayor, Witnesses), processes lifecycle requests,
and handles escalations. Created as wisps (ephemeral, not synced to git).

CHILDREN
  ↳ ✓ skogtown-wisp-wisp-bcnin5: Check inbox for lifecycle requests and messages ● P2
  ↳ ✓ skogtown-wisp-wisp-be8r6i: Decide: loop or hand off ● P2
  ↳ ✓ skogtown-wisp-wisp-k7parg: Scan agent health ● P2
  ↳ ✓ skogtown-wisp-wisp-w5fdbp: Check for orphaned processes ● P2

content.thinking: All children of this patrol wisp are marked as completed (✓). This means the patrol cycle is done. I should check `bd mol current` to confirm there's no remaining step, then squash and decide whether to loop or exit.

content.text: All steps complete on this patrol wisp. Checking for current step and deciding next action.

content.thinking:
Interesting - `bd mol current` shows a different molecule (skogtown-wisp-wisp-09wprr) than what's on my hook (skogtown-wisp-wisp-07qybh). Both have all 4 steps complete. The hook has the old one. Let me squash and create a new patrol.

Actually, looking more carefully - the hooked wisp is 07qybh but mol current shows 09wprr. Both are complete. I should squash the current one and start fresh. Let me squash and create a new patrol.

content.text: Both patrol wisps are fully complete. Squashing and starting fresh.

input.command: gt mol squash --jitter 10s --summary "Patrol complete: all steps done from previous session"
